evt_second_reality.name: Second Reality
evt_second_reality.headline: PC RENDERING MASTERPIECE DEBUTS AT ASSEMBLY
evt_second_reality.description: Undisputed masterpiece of PC rendering history — voxel mountains, rotating spheres, and a haunting chiptune soundtrack by Purple Motion and Skaven. Sets the bar for every PC demo crew for the next five years.

evt_state_of_the_art.name: State of the Art
evt_state_of_the_art.headline: STYLISTIC VECTOR ANIMATION STUNS THE SCENE
evt_state_of_the_art.description: Stylistic Amiga vector animation display that shocked the scene by emphasising direction and techno beats over raw assembler maths. A turning point for Amiga storytelling.

evt_hardwired.name: Hardwired
evt_hardwired.headline: AMIGA HARDWARE SCALING REVEALED
evt_hardwired.description: Incredible Amiga presentation of dynamic scaling, zoom routines, and copper-driven vector polygons. Defines the late-16-bit aesthetic for years.

evt_lifeforce.name: Lifeforce
evt_lifeforce.headline: COPPER AUTOMATION REIGNS
evt_lifeforce.description: A late-16-bit entrance by Andromeda showcasing heavy copper-list automation and a creeping chiptune soundtrack. A masterclass in period timing tricks.

evt_panic.name: Panic
evt_panic.headline: FLAT FRAME BUFFERS PROVE THEIR WORTH
evt_panic.description: High-speed VGA effects that demonstrated what raw PC CPUs could do without custom maths chipsets. A Future Crew warm-up before their magnum opus.

evt_werkzeug.name: .fr-08: Werkzeug
evt_werkzeug.headline: PROCEDURAL 64K MASTERPIECE
evt_werkzeug.description: Gorgeous 64KB masterpiece with procedural music synthesizers and full procedural texture rendering. The benchmark every modern 4K/64K crew chases.

evt_deus_ex_machina.name: Deus Ex Machina
evt_deus_ex_machina.headline: 8-BIT VOXELS PUSH THE 1MHZ LIMIT
evt_deus_ex_machina.description: Pushed 1MHz 6502 assembler to render volumetric pixel fields and fluid demo loops far beyond the C64's norms. A modern miracle of cycle counting.

evt_breakpoint_2000.name: Breakpoint 2000
evt_breakpoint_2000.headline: AMIGA SCENE GATHERS IN BINGEN
evt_breakpoint_2000.description: Massive Easter demoparty with heavy Amiga representation. Hosts world-class 64KB intro and mega-demo competitions.

evt_assembly_1993.name: Assembly Summer 1993
evt_assembly_1993.headline: SECOND REALITY PREMIERE
evt_assembly_1993.description: The Assembly Summer 1993 finals saw Future Crew's Second Reality sweep the PC mega-demo competition and redefine the art form.

evt_bbs_raster_war.name: BBS Raster War
evt_bbs_raster_war.headline: FLAME WAR ERUPTS OVER CYCLE-ACCURATE RASTER TIMING
evt_bbs_raster_war.description: Heated BBS forum debate over who owns the canonical raster-timing macro. Modems across Europe glow red overnight as sysops try to keep the peace.

evt_fc_plagiarism_rumor.name: Future Crew Plagiarism Rumor
evt_fc_plagiarism_rumor.headline: WHISPERS OF STOLEN MATRIX ROUTINES
evt_fc_plagiarism_rumor.description: Anonymous BBS posts claim Future Crew borrowed matrix-rotation hacks from an obscure Eastern Bloc crew. The crew's coders respond with decompiled source dumps.

evt_sid_wizard_release.name: SID-Wizard Released
evt_sid_wizard_release.headline: FIRST TRACKER-STYLE SID COMPOSER
evt_sid_wizard_release.description: Tracker-style SID composer with envelope arpeggio macros ships to the BBS network. Loops SID through ADSR sweeps in a way no prior tool could match.

evt_amiga_format_announce.name: Amiga Format Magazine Issue 1
evt_amiga_format_announce.headline: DEMOSCENE REACHES NEWSSTANDS
evt_amiga_format_announce.description: First major mainstream magazine to dedicate a regular column to the demoscene. Brings the underground conversation to a wider readership and sparks a wave of new joiners.
